summary_one_line: Input must parse as a valid protocol/URL so curl fetches body bytes, making k->bytecount nonzero on the second loop iteration

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed protocol scheme + host:port + HTTP response prelude (e.g. the literal byte runs 'pop3'/'HTTP' at offsets 0x06-0x09, '127.0.0.1:9001' / ':900' region 0x0a-0x1f, and 'GHTTP/' at 0x24-0x29) so curl establishes a transfer, writes body bytes, and on the next read-loop iteration k->bytecount is already nonzero, taking the FALSE side of `if(!k->bytecount)`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry intact literal protocol tokens — 'pop3'/'pop' and 'HTTP' magic at fixed offsets and a coherent host:port string — so curl actually downloads body content (k->bytecount += nread at line 776 is reached, marked [W]) and re-enters the loop with bytecount>0; the false branch is hit (T=20 F=23 on line 585 for W vs T=20 F=0 for L).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are dominated by '%' filler bytes (0x25 repeated across offsets 0x06-0x3e) with no coherent scheme/host literals, so the parse never reaches the body-write path (the entire 0x599+ body-write region is L-blank), bytecount stays 0, and only the TRUE side of line 585 is ever taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer of intercepted scheme/host/HTTP-prelude comparisons and substitutes the exact expected operands ('pop3', 'HTTP', port digits) into the input, synthesizing a parseable request in far fewer execs than blind havoc; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot reconstruct these multi-byte literals, so they never advance to the body-write that increments bytecount.

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs are the I2S technique delta and the winner resolves 10/10 while the loser blocks 10/10.
The second I2S pair (vpc vs vp) shows the same complete resolution divergence.
The blocker is the false side of an equality-to-zero test on the running byte counter.
The blocked side is the false branch (bytecount must be nonzero).
Winner seeds carry intact protocol literals pop3/HTTP and a host:port string at fixed offsets.
Winner seed includes the host:port literal 127.0.0.1:9001 region.
Winner reaches the body-accumulation line k->bytecount += nread, marked W-only.
For the winner the false side of the blocker is taken (F=23) whereas for the loser it is never taken (F=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: L 585  T=20 F=23  T=20 F=0  if(!k->bytecount) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser seeds are dominated by repeated '%' filler bytes with no coherent literals.
At the magic offsets the winner bytes are concentrated protocol literals while the loser bytes are scattered '%' garbage.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes the matched CMP operand into the input at the matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S stage so cannot reconstruct multi-byte CMP operands.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, only the havoc + token stack.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 422 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 21cecf684c1b463d` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 10 and 11 of the 12-level ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where naive/value_profile reach the false side of `if(!k->bytecount)` (i.e. accumulate body bytes) about as fast as cmplog/vpc despite the scheme/host/HTTP-prelude being multi-byte exact-match literals — showing the body-write gate is not I2S-gated.

weakest_evidence_point: I attribute the bytecount increment to a clean protocol parse, but the path from input bytes to k->bytecount += nread spans many intermediate gates (scheme dispatch, header parsing) that the prompt does not byte-trace, so the precise literal that I2S must solve is inferred rather than pinpointed.
